---
title: "TikTok Song Virality Predictor"
author: "Kainoa Pajo"
date: "2026-01-29"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(vip)
```

## Data Simulation

```{r}
set.seed(456)
n_songs <- 4000

tiktok_data <- tibble(
  track_id = 1:n_songs,
  # Audio feature generation (0.0 - 1.0 scale)
  danceability = rbeta(n_songs, 5, 2), 
  energy = rbeta(n_songs, 5, 2),
  valence = rbeta(n_songs, 3, 3),      
  speechiness = rbeta(n_songs, 1, 10), 
  
  # Duration distribution (Minutes)
  duration_min = rnorm(n_songs, mean = 3.5, sd = 0.5)
) %>%
  # Target Variable Definition: Viral Probability Logic
  mutate(
    viral_score = (danceability * 0.4) + (energy * 0.3) + ((4 - duration_min) * 0.3),
    viral_score = viral_score + rnorm(n_songs, 0, 0.1), # Variance injection
    is_viral = ifelse(viral_score > quantile(viral_score, 0.50), "Viral", "Non-Viral"),
    is_viral = as.factor(is_viral)
  )

# Export processed data for external dashboarding
write_csv(tiktok_data, "tiktok_audio_features.csv")
```

## Classification Modeling

```{r}
# Train/Test Split (Stratified)
set.seed(123)
split <- initial_split(tiktok_data, prop = 0.75, strata = is_viral)
train <- training(split)
test  <- testing(split)

# Feature Engineering Recipe
music_recipe <- recipe(is_viral ~ danceability + energy + valence + duration_min, data = train)

# Model 1: Logistic Regression
glm_spec <- logistic_reg() %>%
  set_engine("glm")

# Model 2: Random Forest
rf_spec <- rand_forest(trees = 100) %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity") %>%
  set_mode("classification")

# Workflow Configuration
wf <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(music_recipe) %>%
  add_model(rf_spec)

# Model Fitting
fit <- wf %>% fit(data = train)
```

## Feature Importance Analysis

```{r}
fit %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  vip() +
  labs(title = "Feature Importance: Drivers of Viral Potential", 
       x = "Variable", 
       y = "Importance Score")
```
